long_answer:
<initial_plan>
1) Hard Constraints:
   - Plan a 5-day family vacation to Europe.
   - Budget around €1,700.
   - Must include flights, accommodation, restaurant bookings, and activities that match the user's interests.
   - Send an out-of-office notification to team@globalbiz.com for the vacation period.
   
2) Soft Constraints:
   - The user's interests (implied preferences) should be taken into account for restaurant and activity choices; these can be adjusted based on available options.
   - Flexibility in destination within Europe may be allowed if required to meet the budget and family-friendly criteria.
   - Preferred flight, hotel, and activity quality might be flexible as long as they fit within the budget and family requirements.
   
3) Task Decomposition:
   a) Determine the specifics of the vacation:
      - Confirm the exact travel dates (if not provided, ask for preferred dates).
      - Identify family member count and any specific needs (if available from user's records).
   b) Choose a destination in Europe that is family-friendly and fits the budget.
   c) Book flights:
      - Get flight options including costs and timings that work with the travel dates.
   d) Book accommodation:
      - Identify family-friendly hotels or vacation rentals, check availability and costs.
   e) Plan restaurant bookings:
      - Find suitable dining options (breakfast, lunch, dinner) that match the family's interests.
   f) Plan activities:
      - Identify key attractions or family activities at the destination with costs.
   g) Additional Services:
      - Consider any extra services like travel insurance, transfers, etc., if needed.
   h) Overall Budget Breakdown:
      - Create a complete cost breakdown to ensure the total is around €1,700.
   i) Send out-of-office email:
      - Draft and send an out-of-office notification to team@globalbiz.com indicating the vacation period.
      
4) Data Planning:
   - Request trip travel dates from the environment (user’s calendar or past preferences, if available).
   - Fetch user’s family details from the environment (if available) to ensure appropriate accommodations.
   - Retrieve user’s business email details from the environment to confirm the out-of-office message details.
   - Communicate with the external travel agency to:
       • Provide destination options considering family-friendliness and budget.
       • Get flight options with costs.
       • Check accommodation options with costs.
       • Get recommendations for restaurants and local activities.
       • Provide a complete breakdown of the total cost including additional services.
       
5) Anticipated Changes:
   - Travel dates might need adjustment based on availability or family schedule.
   - Destination choice and activity options may be flexible based on cost, seasonal factors, or agency recommendations.
   - Final cost breakdown may require minor revisions to stick to the budget if certain components exceed limits.
   - The out-of-office message content might be refined based on preferred vacation dates and tone.
   
Overall, all details such as destination, dates, transportation, accommodation, activities, additional services, and total budget must be finalized with exact values. We will interact with both the environment and the external travel agency to ensure all data is complete and consistent.
</initial_plan>

long_answer:
<output_to_ai_assistant>
Below is our complete set of options for your family vacation in Lisbon (as an alternative to Algarve) for a 5-day period (for example, July 1–5, 2024):

1. Restaurant Recommendations:
   • Italian Trattoria – Enjoy authentic pasta and pizza in a relaxed, family-friendly atmosphere. Estimated meal costs are approximately €20–€45 per person, and they offer early dining specials on weekdays.
   • Vegan Bistro – Ideal for varied dietary preferences with a casual ambiance. Costs are approximately €20–€35 per person. Reservations for groups can secure a set menu deal.
   • Seafood Shack – Perfect for sampling local fresh catches, with menu prices estimated around €30–€60 per person. They often provide family-style platters with a fixed group price.
   • French Café – For a quieter dining option offering pastries and coffees; estimated costs are around €10–€25 per person.
   We recommend contacting these venues early to secure group discounts or reservation deals, particularly for the Italian Trattoria which has been popular for family visits.

2. Local Family-Friendly Activities/Attractions:
   • City Walking Tour – A guided tour of Lisbon’s historic sites. Cost: €20 per person.
   • Museum Day Pass – Access multiple museums in Lisbon and learn about local art and history. Cost: €30 per person.
   • Night City Tour – An evening excursion to see the city’s illuminated landmarks. Cost: €25 per person.
   • Public Aquarium Visit – Lisbon’s renowned aquarium visit is ideal for families; entrance is approximately €25 per person.
   • Zoo Visit – Enjoy a day out with the kids; costs are around €15 per child and €25 per adult.
   (Note: Depending on the family’s choices, group discounts may be available when booking multiple activities.)

3. Complete Cost Breakdown (approximate figures for a family of four):

Below is our complete set of options for your family vacation in Lisbon (as an alternative to Algarve) for a 5-day period (for example, July 1–5, 2024):

1. Restaurant Recommendations:
   • Italian Trattoria – Enjoy authentic pasta and pizza in a relaxed, family-friendly atmosphere. Estimated meal costs are approximately €20–€45 per person, and they offer early dining specials on weekdays.
   • Vegan Bistro – Ideal for varied dietary preferences with a casual ambiance. Costs are approximately €20–€35 per person. Reservations for groups can secure a set menu deal.
   • Seafood Shack – Perfect for sampling local fresh catches, with menu prices estimated around €30–€60 per person. They often provide family-style platters with a fixed group price.
   • French Café – For a quieter dining option offering pastries and coffees; estimated costs are around €10–€25 per person.
   We recommend contacting these venues early to secure group discounts or reservation deals, particularly for the Italian Trattoria which has been popular for family visits.

2. Local Family-Friendly Activities/Attractions:
   • City Walking Tour – A guided tour of Lisbon’s historic sites. Cost: €20 per person.
   • Museum Day Pass – Access multiple museums in Lisbon and learn about local art and history. Cost: €30 per person.
   • Night City Tour – An evening excursion to see the city’s illuminated landmarks. Cost: €25 per person.
   • Public Aquarium Visit – Lisbon’s renowned aquarium visit is ideal for families; entrance is approximately €25 per person.
   • Zoo Visit – Enjoy a day out with the kids; costs are around €15 per child and €25 per adult.
   (Note: Depending on the family’s choices, group discounts may be available when booking multiple activities.)
